Simulate Factor Analysis Data with Known Parameters

Source: R/simulate_latent.R
simulate_fa.Rd

Generate multivariate normal data from an explicit factor model for factor analysis parameter recovery testing. The implied covariance matrix \(\Sigma = \Lambda \Phi \Lambda' + \Psi\) is fully specified and stored in the return value for direct comparison with estimated covariances.

Usage

simulate_fa(loadings, phi = NULL, psi = NULL, n, seed = NULL)

Arguments

loadings

Numeric matrix of dimension p x m (observed variables x factors). Must be a matrix (not a vector). Zero entries indicate no loading; use matrix(val, nrow = p, ncol = 1) for single-factor models.

phi

Numeric matrix of dimension m x m. Factor correlation matrix. Must be symmetric and positive-definite. Defaults to diag(m) (orthogonal factors).

psi

Numeric vector of length p. Unique variances. All values must be positive. Defaults to 1 - diag(loadings %*% phi %*% t(loadings)) (communality complement). An error is raised if any auto-computed value is \(\le 0\) (over-factored model).

n

Positive integer. Sample size.

seed

Integer or NULL. Random seed.

Value

A named list with elements:

data

data.frame with columns y1...yp.

params

list with loadings, phi, psi, and sigma_implied (the \(p x p\) model-implied covariance matrix).

Examples

# Two orthogonal factors, 6 indicators (3 per factor)
loadings <- matrix(c(0.8, 0.7, 0.6, 0,   0,   0,
                     0,   0,   0,   0.8, 0.7, 0.6),
                   nrow = 6, ncol = 2)  # byrow = FALSE (default)
r <- simulate_fa(loadings = loadings, n = 500, seed = 1)
r$data                  # data.frame: y1..y6
#>               y1            y2            y3           y4           y5
#> 1   -0.626453811 -0.2867690683  0.6872958302  0.850043472 -0.258144036
#> 2    0.183643324 -0.1431132137  0.9880917992 -0.925312995 -2.110749248
#> 3   -0.835628612 -1.4482594715 -1.3642366102  0.893581214  1.842315572
#> 4    1.595280802  0.9027131581  0.9486193298 -0.941009738 -0.096754176
#> 5    0.329507772  1.0060584798  0.3986777489  0.538952094  0.255541917
#> 6   -0.820468384  0.8611279564 -1.5296039570 -0.181974398  0.475071178
#> 7    0.487429052 -0.8643208703  0.6792079626  0.891767595  0.543727197
#> 8    0.738324705  0.2066610222 -1.3320954007  1.329208205 -0.341203596

r$params$sigma_implied  # implied covariance matrix
#>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] [,6]
#> [1,] 1.00 0.56 0.48 0.00 0.00 0.00
#> [2,] 0.56 1.00 0.42 0.00 0.00 0.00
#> [3,] 0.48 0.42 1.00 0.00 0.00 0.00
#> [4,] 0.00 0.00 0.00 1.00 0.56 0.48
#> [5,] 0.00 0.00 0.00 0.56 1.00 0.42
#> [6,] 0.00 0.00 0.00 0.48 0.42 1.00

# Oblique model
phi <- matrix(c(1, 0.4, 0.4, 1), nrow = 2)
r2  <- simulate_fa(loadings = loadings, phi = phi, n = 500, seed = 1)
